rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the FLAW at the memcpy call around line 42 using sizeof(structCharVoid), explaining it copies 32 bytes into a 16-byte charFirst and overwrites voidSecond/voidThird. They described the overflow mechanism and resulting corrupted pointer dereference.
rationale.local_fix: They explicitly replaced sizeof(structCharVoid) with sizeof(structCharVoid.charFirst) in the memcpy length. This matches the required proximate fix exactly.
rationale.global_fix: They listed multiple generic options (strncpy, wrapper, std::array, tools) without clearly committing to representing the destination as a bounded buffer/span in this code. No concrete change tying copy length to the field’s capacity via a bounded type was specified.
